user_text:
The process begins when a guest calls room service. The room service manager answers the call and takes down the guest's order. After noting the order, the manager submits an order ticket to the kitchen. Simultaneously, the manager gives the order details to the sommelier and assigns the order to a waiter.

At this point, the process splits into parallel activities. The kitchen and the sommelier start working on their respective tasks to prepare the order. Meanwhile, the waiter prepares a cart for delivery. Once both the kitchen and sommelier have completed their tasks and the waiter has readied the cart, the process continues.

The waiter then delivers the cart to the guest's room. After delivering the cart, the waiter returns to the room-service station. At this stage, the process encounters a decision point.

If the guest's account needs to be debited, the waiter proceeds to debit the guest's account. If not, the waiter waits to do the billing at a later time. Once either of these actions is completed, the process comes to an end.
output_text:
The process begins when a guest calls room service. The room service manager answers the call and takes down the guest's order. After noting the order, the manager submits an order ticket to the kitchen to prepare the requested items. Simultaneously, the manager gives the order details to the sommelier if any beverages are required. Additionally, the manager assigns the order to a waiter who will be responsible for its delivery.

Once the order is assigned, two activities happen at the same time. In one activity, the kitchen staff and the sommelier work on preparing the food and drinks as per the order. In the other activity, the waiter prepares a cart to carry the items to the guest's room. Both of these activities are completed independently but concurrently.

After both the kitchen and sommelier have completed their tasks and the waiter has readied the cart, the waiter proceeds to deliver the cart to the guest's room. Once the delivery is made, the waiter returns to the room-service station.

Back at the station, the waiter waits for the appropriate time to handle the billing process. When it is time, the waiter debits the guest's account for the services provided. This marks the end of the process.
